title: Detoxifying Large Language Models via Autoregressive Reward Guided Representation Editing

repos.name: xiaoyisong/ARGRE
repos.evidence: README titled 'Code of ARGRE'; repo owner 'xiaoyisong' = lead author Yisong Xiao (Beihang). Layout mirrors the paper's method exactly: reward_model/ (My{Llama,GPT2,Mistral,OPT}ForCausalLM autoregressive reward models), evaluation/collect_hidden (PCA non-toxic direction), evaluation/train_hidden (train reward model), evaluation/baselines/run_argre.py (two-step steer+gradient editing). run_argre.py argparse description='ARGRE'; configs cover the 8 LLMs from the paper (gpt2/opt-6.7b/mistral/llama-7b/13b/30b/sft).

repos.name: unitaryai/detoxify
repos.evidence: README '🙊 Detoxify - Toxic Comment Classification', built by Laura Hanu at Unitary (citation year 2020, Jigsaw Kaggle challenges) - not by the paper's authors and not the ARGRE method. Used purely as the toxicity-scoring evaluator: pinned as 'detoxify==0.5.2' in ARGRE/req.txt and called via Detoxify('original', ...) in evaluation/utils/evaluate_model.py.

rationale: The author core IS present: xiaoyisong/ARGRE (owner = lead author Yisong Xiao, README 'Code of ARGRE') implements the paper's named method end-to-end (PCA non-toxic direction, autoregressive reward model, two-step steer+gradient editing) across the paper's 8 LLMs. A second repo, unitaryai/detoxify, is a third-party toxicity classifier (Unitary/Laura Hanu, 2020) used only as the evaluation scorer and pinned dependency, not this paper's method - so it is an extra non-core repo cloned alongside the core.
audit_impact: No misattribution. All six findings in findings_verified.json are written against the core ARGRE repo and are legitimate reproducibility/bug concerns (missing pairwise training data split_0/1.jsonl, missing trained reward checkpoints/exp outputs, missing Section 5 stereotype/jailbreak code, deps absent from req.txt, 10-token eval). None claim 'no code exists'. The 'detoxify-checkpoint-placeholder-path' finding correctly treats detoxify as the external scorer (placeholder path 'todo/toxic_original-c1212f89.ckpt'), so the extra repo did not corrupt any verdict.
recommended_action: Keep the paper as source-present (core = xiaoyisong/ARGRE). Tag unitaryai/detoxify as a third-party evaluation dependency, not author code, so it is not counted as a separate author release. Existing findings stand.
